name: Azure OpenAI Service MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Azure OpenAI Service — Microsoft's enterprise deployment of OpenAI models (GPT-4, GPT-4o, GPT-4 Turbo, Whisper, DALL-E) with enhanced compliance, private networking, and data processing guarantees. Enables AI agents to call Azure-hosted OpenAI models with EU/US data residency, private endpoints, and Microsoft's enterprise SLA.
use_cases: Calling GPT-4 models in enterprise environments with data residency requirements via agents
Processing sensitive documents and data with EU GDPR compliance via enterprise AI agents
Integrating OpenAI models within Azure Virtual Networks for secure agent deployments
Building compliant AI applications for regulated industries (healthcare, finance, government)
not_for: Consumer or startup use cases (Azure OpenAI is enterprise-focused with approval process)
Use cases where standard OpenAI API is sufficient (Azure adds complexity)
best_when: An enterprise in a regulated industry or with EU data residency requirements wants GPT-4 models with Microsoft's enterprise compliance guarantees, private networking, and Azure identity management.
avoid_when: You can use standard OpenAI API — Azure OpenAI requires Azure subscription and approval. FINANCIAL RISK: Azure AI costs bill to Azure subscription.

auth.notes: Azure AD (Entra ID) managed identity or API key. Managed identity preferred for production agents. API keys available for development. Azure RBAC controls model access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; Azure subscription required
pricing.paid_tiers: Per token pricing similar to OpenAI; GPT-4o ~$5/1M input tokens
pricing.notes: Azure subscription required. Token pricing similar to direct OpenAI. Azure adds enterprise features at marginal cost overhead.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per model deployment; configurable TPM (tokens per minute)

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Azure AI costs bill to Azure subscription — implement cost controls
Azure OpenAI requires Microsoft approval — apply in advance for access
Model deployment names must be configured per Azure resource — not standard model names
Private endpoint configuration adds significant setup complexity for agents
Azure AD authentication is complex — managed identity recommended over API keys
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: LLM costs bill to Azure. Enterprise compliance (HIPAA, FedRAMP, GDPR, EU AI Act). Azure AD auth with managed identity. Private networking available.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise AI with HIPAA, FedRAMP, SOC1/2, GDPR, EU AI Act compliance. Azure AD managed identity. Private endpoints. Data residency by region.
